name: ignix-ui
description: MCP tool definitions for Ignix UI CLI - A React component library with 90+ components, 200+ themes, and 20+ templates

metadata.totalComponents: DYNAMIC (use list_components to get current count)
metadata.totalThemes: DYNAMIC (use list_themes to get current count)
metadata.totalTemplates: DYNAMIC (use list_templates to get current count)

instructions.forAI: IMPORTANT: This library is CONSTANTLY GROWING. Never assume you know all available components/themes/templates. ALWAYS use the list/search tools FIRST to discover what's available at the moment.
instructions.discoveryFirst: Before suggesting any component, theme, or template, ALWAYS call the appropriate list/search tool to check current availability. Do NOT rely on memory or documentation alone.
instructions.growthMindset: New components, themes, and templates are added regularly. What was available last week may have expanded this week. Always discover first, then act.

workflow.initialization: Always check if ignix.config.js exists. If not, call init_project first.
workflow.discovery: BEFORE any installation, ALWAYS call search_components or list_components to verify component existence. NEVER guess component names.
workflow.installation: ALWAYS use install_component for adding components. NEVER create component files manually.
workflow.theming: BEFORE installing a theme, ALWAYS call list_themes to see current available themes.
workflow.templates: For complete pages, ALWAYS call list_templates first to see available templates.

emptyDirectory.detection: ALWAYS check if the current directory is empty or doesn't contain a React project (no package.json or package.json without react dependency).
emptyDirectory.action: If directory is empty or no React project detected, ALWAYS run create_react_project FIRST, THEN init_project, THEN proceed with the requested installation.
emptyDirectory.example: For 'build me a landing page' in empty folder: 1. create_react_project({ projectName: 'gym-landing' }) → 2. init_project({ cwd: './gym-landing' }) → 3. search_templates for 'landing' to find page templates → 4. install_template with templates (hero, callToAction) → 5. search_components for UI elements → 6. install_component with components (navbar, button, card, footer)

tools.name: create_react_project
tools.description: [PROJECT CREATION] Creates a new React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS project using Vite.

execution.command: cd {cwd} && npm create vite@latest {projectName} -- --template react-ts && cd {projectName} && npm install && npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer && npx tailwindcss init -p && echo '@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;' > src/index.css

tools.name: list_components
tools.description: [DISCOVERY] Returns paginated list of available components (15 per page).

tools.name: add_component
tools.description: [INSTALLATION] Installs Ignix UI components to your project.

tools.name: generate_template
tools.description: [PAGE GENERATION] Generates a complete page template using Ignix UI components.

tools.name: validate_ignix_only
tools.description: [VALIDATION] Scans codebase to ensure only Ignix UI components are used. Rejects shadcn, MUI, Chakra, AntD.

execution.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const patterns=[/@shadcn\/ui/,/@mui\/material/,/@chakra-ui/,/@ant-design/]; let violations=[]; function scan(dir){if(!fs.existsSync(dir))return; const files=fs.readdirSync(dir); for(const file of files){const filePath=path.join(dir,file); if(fs.statSync(filePath).isDirectory()){if(!['node_modules','.next','dist','build'].includes(file))scan(filePath);}else if(/\.(tsx?|jsx?)$/.test(file)){const content=fs.readFileSync(filePath,'utf8'); for(const pattern of patterns){if(pattern.test(content)){violations.push(filePath+': '+pattern.source);}}}}} scan(path.join('{cwd}','{path}')); console.log(JSON.stringify({success:violations.length===0,valid:violations.length===0,violations,count:violations.length,classification:violations.length?'VALIDATION_FAILED':'VALIDATION_PASSED',action:violations.length?'Replace these imports with Ignix UI components':null}));"

tools.name: get_component_docs
tools.description: [DOCUMENTATION] Fetches live documentation for a component on-demand. Token-efficient.

execution.command: node -e "fetch(`https://mindfiredigital.github.io/ignix-ui/api/docs/{component}.json`).then(r=>r.ok?r.json():Promise.reject()).then(docs=>console.log(JSON.stringify({success:true,component:'{component}',docs:{description:docs.description,props:docs.props,examples:docs.examples,usage:docs.usage},tokenCount:Math.ceil(JSON.stringify(docs).length/4)}))).catch(()=>console.log(JSON.stringify({success:false,error:'Documentation not found',classification:'DOCS_NOT_FOUND',action:'Use list_components to verify component exists'})))"

resources.name: component_registry
resources.description: Machine-readable component registry containing all available components

resources.name: documentation
resources.description: AI-readable documentation with usage guidelines, workflows, and examples

resources.name: ignix-rules
resources.description: Strict enforcement rules for Ignix UI (under 200 tokens)

resources.name: component-registry-summary
resources.description: Summary of available components from the registry
